Listings & financings history.

Eighteen years of TSX and TSXV new listings and the capital raised behind them.
Jump toChartRosterFinancingsMethod
03 Listings & financings history

Eighteen years of new listings — and who is still here.

Every new listing on the TSX and TSXV since 2008, stacked by how it came to market, with a click-through to the actual companies and what became of them. Then the capital side: total equity financing per year, and a name-by-name search of the corporate financings the TMX MiG Reports singled out each month. Composition and board toggles recompute the bars; click any year to open its roster.

Board Composition
Measure
Stack by
Types

New listings by year — Combined, all listings

Stacked by listing type · 2008–2025
IPO CPC / qualifying TSXV graduation ETF-ETP / fund Other (RTO / listing)
Stacked by sector. On the Listings (count) measure the bars are the roster's sector field — top-6 sectors + Other (the mining-dominance / tech-thinness story made visual); board, composition and type filters all still apply. On All capital raised (C$) the bars switch to MiG sector-capital (canonicalized, 2013 onward): top-6 sectors by dollars + an Other & unattributed residual that reconciles the stack exactly to the headline capital-raised total. Follow-on and IPO measures have no reconciled sector split — the chart falls back to the aggregate bar there. sector-capital method →
Hover, tap or focus any bar for its year, board scope and per-type breakdown.
current selection recomputes on every board / type / measure change
Reading these bars — the two stat bands & the type taxonomy
Two bands, two jobs. The dashed-outline current-selection band above recomputes live from the board + type filters and the measure. The solid Reconciliation strip below stays pinned to the canonical unfiltered anchors — it never moves with your filters.
Type taxonomy shifts across eras — the 2008–11 MiG basis bundled CPC IPOs into a single "IPO incl. CPC" row; the modern split (IPO / CPC-qualifying / graduation / ETF-ETP / other) is applied consistently here from the per-company archive roster. The ETF-ETP surge visible from ~2021 is product creation on the TSX, not operating-company listings — switch to Operating only to strip it.

Reconciliation

The module's totals against the canonical counts, where they meet
225
2024 new listings
TSX 150 · TSXV 75 · roster + MiG agree
445
2025 new listings
TSX 373 · TSXV 72 · per-company archive roster
211
2026 YTD · to 31 May
TSX 178 · TSXV 33 · MiG headline (live)
MiG 449
2025 · December-YTD basis
The MiG event count logs 449 for 2025 vs the roster's 445 — the +4 is same-year churn the year-end files miss. Directional, documented.
data & method

The TSXV→TSX escalator — graduations per year

The graduation stock the Boards explorer facets, charted as a flow · 2009–2025
Panel transitions — the complete escalator flow (465 total, 13–52/yr) Roster grad-type — partial cross-check (352 total) 224 graduates remain on the TSX today (May 2026)
Reconciling the two graduation sources & the 224 stock
The panel-transition series (migration events detected in the year-end panels) is the complete escalator flow — 465 graduations 2009–2025, 13–52/yr. The roster grad-type series (352) is lower because the archive new-listings file explicitly tags only a subset of graduations as listing-type 'TSXV Grad'; the panel catches every board change. Both track the same cyclical shape (2011 and 2021 peaks). Of the cumulative flow, 224 graduates remain on the TSX operating basis as of 31 May 2026 (the rest have since delisted, been acquired or moved on) — the static 224-graduate stock the Boards explorer facets.
Two sources, one escalator. The panel-transition series counts every TSXV→TSX board change detected in the year-end panels — the fuller, primary flow (13–52/yr, matching the champions-report range). The roster grad-type series counts only the new-TSX listings the archive explicitly tagged listing-type “TSXV Grad,” so it undercounts; both track the same cyclical shape (2011 and 2021 peaks). For the escalator as a policy thesis — CPC feeder, sector split of graduates, index inclusion — see the champions report's graduation-pipeline treatment.
data & method

Company roster — 2025

445 listings · status computed by joining the archive panel to the May-2026 snapshot
Listing type
Filters sync with the chart. The Listing type chips here are the same set as the chart's — toggling either updates both. Operating companies only = every listing except ETF-ETP/fund creations (the same partition as the chart's Operating only composition). Clicking a year on the chart while type filters are active opens that year's roster already narrowed to those types.

Deal amount vs market cap. Deal amount is the money raised in the listing-year offering, shown only where the TMX MiG named the issuer among a month's ten largest financings (tagged MiG top-10); TMX publishes per-deal amounts only for that Top-10, so most rows read — — never zero. Mkt cap is the company's value at the December year-end of its listing year (from the archive workbook), not the deal size. Value sort uses the deal amount where present, falling back to market cap.
Status join — directional
"Still listed" is a name match to the 31 May 2026 TMX snapshot (robust to the Shopify SH→SHOP ticker change and recycled tickers such as HUT). Graduations and disappearances come from the archive panel transitions (ticker|board with a name fallback). "Delisted by 20XX" means the issuer had left the panel by that year-end — no reason is attributed; the archive leaves it blank by design. Never read it as went-private, acquired or failed.
data & method

Financings — total equity capital raised

Combined TSX + TSXV, December-YTD · 2009–2026 (2026 is YTD, drawn dashed)

Named financings — search by issuer

The monthly Top-10 corporate financings the MiG named, per board · 2013 onward

cross-listed cut named-financing issuers that are interlisted abroad today
161 of 1,528 named-financing issuers currently interlisted abroad821 named deals by them (Top-10 basis)status as-of May 2026 — see the boards Cross-listings section for the deal-year (as-of-date) cut
Shopify Inc.
23 named financings · C$27,026M total (Top-10 basis) · example
C$187M · IPO 2015-05 · TSXC$302M · Public offering 2016-08 · TSXC$302M · Public offering 2016-09 · TSXC$776M · Public offering 2017-05 · TSXC$776M · Public offering 2017-06 · TSXC$776M · Public offering 2017-12 · TSXC$836M · Public offering 2018-02 · TSXC$836M · Public offering 2018-03 · TSXC$536M · Public offering 2018-12 · TSXC$536M · Public offering 2018-12 · TSXC$921M · Public offering 2019-09 · TSXC$921M · Public offering 2019-09 · TSXC$921M · Public offering 2019-12 · TSXC$2,100M · Public offering 2020-05 · TSXC$2,100M · Public offering 2020-06 · TSXC$1,500M · Public offering 2020-09 · TSXC$1,500M · Public offering 2020-09 · TSXC$2,100M · Public offering 2020-12 · TSXC$1,500M · Public offering 2020-12 · TSXC$1,900M · Public offering 2021-02 · TSXC$1,900M · Public offering 2021-03 · TSXC$1,900M · Public offering 2021-06 · TSXC$1,900M · Public offering 2021-12 · TSX
Top-10s only — an honest floor, not the full tape. The MiG named just the ten largest corporate financings per board each month (from 2013). The long tail of smaller placements is captured only in the yearly C$ total above, not here; and a single large deal can recur across consecutive months' rankings. Confidence: medium-high on the named deals, high on the yearly totals.
data & method
◇ Data & method

What this page's numbers rest on.

Only the load-bearing basis for the figures above. The full appendix — every source, entity basis, gap-fill convention, the FX table and the refresh chain — lives on the method page.

Listings & financings basis
roster + MiG
Annual bars are the year-end archive roster (5,873 rows); the MiG December-YTD table is the type×board cross-check. Deal amount is a partial join (MiG names only each month's ten largest financings, so most rows read —). No reason is attributed to any disappearance.
Vintages
as-at dates
TMX Issuers List 31 May 2026 · CSE 31 Dec 2025 · WFE export 8 Jul 2026. Gap-filled/partial points are dashed with a †/*.

Sources for this page

RoleFile
Listings history — archiveData/tmx-archive-derived/ (new_listings_by_year.csv 5,873 · panel_transitions.csv · issuer_panel.csv — 18 year-end TMX Listed-Companies + New-Listings workbooks, Dec 2008-Dec 2025)
Listings history — MiGData/tmx-mig-derived/ (mig_new_listings_by_type.csv · mig_monthly_headlines.csv · mig_named_financings.csv — 167 monthly TMX MiG Reports, Dec 2008-Jun 2026)
Source & confidence Full data & method →
Markets Dashboard · standalone

A self-contained, filter-everything view of Canada's equity boards and 25 world exchanges, built for Ontario capital-markets policy research. Every module carries a source pill — tap it for the figure's provenance and confidence grade.

Data as at: TMX 31 May 2026 · CSE 31 Dec 2025 · WFE export 8 Jul 2026 (market-cap 2026 partial point: latest month reported, May/Jun 2026). Generated by markets_dashboard_data.py + build_markets_dashboard.py.

MARKETS DASHBOARD · static-first · opens from file:// · default view renders with JavaScript off · Mission Control design system
